12		"time"
13	
14		"github.com/amnezia-vpn/amneziawg-go/conn"
15	
16		"github.com/7mind/wanbond/internal/adaptivefec"
17		"github.com/7mind/wanbond/internal/config"
18		"github.com/7mind/wanbond/internal/fec"
19		"github.com/7mind/wanbond/internal/frame"
20		"github.com/7mind/wanbond/internal/reseq"
21		"github.com/7mind/wanbond/internal/sched"
22		"github.com/7mind/wanbond/internal/telemetry"
23	)
24	
25	// socketRecvBuffer is the SO_RCVBUF requested on every per-path socket, matching
26	// wireguard-go's StdNetBind (~7 MiB). A large receive buffer absorbs the bursts a
27	// bonded, reordered flow delivers while the userspace engine catches up; the
28	// pass-through P0 Bind used the OS default and lost datagrams under load (P0
29	// findings §1/§2). The kernel silently caps the request at net.core.rmem_max, so
30	// the effective size may be smaller on an untuned host — best-effort by design.
31	const socketRecvBuffer = 7 << 20
32	
33	// multipathBatchSize is the number of datagrams a ReceiveFunc / Send handles per
34	// call. T12 keeps it at 1 (one syscall per datagram, per path); GSO/GRO batching
35	// is best-effort future work (P0 findings §2) tracked separately and does not
36	// change the wire format.
37	const multipathBatchSize = 1
38	
39	// maxDatagram bounds a single received outer datagram. It comfortably exceeds a
40	// full-MTU inner packet plus every outer/WG/amnezia-junk overhead.
41	const maxDatagram = 65535
42	
43	// Receive-resequencer tuning (T18), sized against P0 findings §6.
44	//
45	// resequencerWindow is the maximum span of outer-seq positions the receive
46	// resequencer buffers while it waits for an out-of-order frame. WG's inner RFC
47	// 6479 anti-replay window is 8128 messages (docs/p0-findings.md §6); the window
48	// here sits ~4x below that, so even a worst-case burst of reordered/held frames
49	// stays comfortably inside the inner filter's tolerance while spanning far more
50	// packets than the measured ~19 ms emulated cross-path skew (and the larger,
51	// more variable real Starlink/5G skew) needs at realistic per-path rates.
52	//
53	// resequencerTimeout bounds how long a head-of-line-blocked run is held for a
54	// missing (presumed-lost) lower frame before that gap is skipped and the run
55	// released. It caps the added latency — and the stall a slow/lossy path can
56	// impose on the whole bond (P0 §7 head-of-line concern) — to a few multiples of
57	// a Starlink RTT (~45 ms) rather than holding a gap forever.
58	const (
59		resequencerWindow  = 2048
60		resequencerTimeout = 250 * time.Millisecond
61	)
62	
63	// defaultMaxDemuxSources caps the source->peer demux map (peerBySource), the
64	// PROVISIONAL/unbound-source tracking state whose growth an attacker probes at
65	// bootstrap (Q26/Q27). It is sized SEPARATELY from the steady-state peer set
66	// (m.peersByName, bounded by the static configured [[wireguard.peers]]): a source
67	// enters this map only on an authenticated PROBE (T88), but a party holding ONE
68	// valid psk could otherwise bind an unbounded number of distinct spoofed sources to
69	// its OWN peer and exhaust memory. On exhaustion a NEW source's binding is dropped
70	// (drop-on-exhaustion, bootstrap degrades) — an already-bound (live) source is NEVER
71	// evicted. Roam/re-bind of an already-present source (T90) does not grow the map, so
72	// it is never blocked by the cap. A dead peer's bindings are reclaimed on teardown
73	// (TearDownPeer), freeing slots. The default is generous relative to any realistic
74	// concentrator's peer×roam-churn count yet bounds the flood surface.
75	const defaultMaxDemuxSources = 1024
76	
77	var (
78		// ErrNoHealthyPath is exported (I4) so the device-package engineLogger adapter can
79		// errors.Is-match it against the engine's wrapped Errorf args to gate the startup
80		// no-healthy-path warmup coalescing without string-matching the log message.
81		ErrNoHealthyPath = errors.New("bind: no healthy path with a known remote endpoint")
82		// errPacerShedding is returned by Send when the scheduler shed the datagram for
83		// pacing (PickPaced) while paths are healthy — deliberate rate limiting, NOT an
84		// outage. It is DISTINCT from ErrNoHealthyPath so operator logs and the e2e
85		// log-grep harness can tell shedding from total path failure (the drop behavior is
86		// identical to the pre-existing no-path case; only the diagnostic differs). The
87		// coalesced, rate-limited "pacer shedding" record is emitted by the scheduler.
88		errPacerShedding = errors.New("bind: datagram shed by send pacer (paths healthy, rate limited)")
89		errClosed        = net.ErrClosed
90	)
91	
92	// sharedPathState is the per-SOCKET state of one configured uplink, SHARED across
93	// every peer bound beneath the Bind: its stable path-id, source address, and the
94	// source-bound UDP socket (drained by exactly one Bind-owned readLoop). A concentrator
95	// front-ends many peers on the SAME socket, so this identity is owned once per socket
96	// and referenced by every peer's per-(peer,path) view (peerPathState). On the single-peer
97	// edge/hub there is exactly one peer, so each shared path has exactly one peerPathState.
98	// The deferred-path machinery (deferredPath, m.deferred) likewise operates on this shared
99	// socket layer — a runtime add/remove of a shared path fans the per-(peer,path) state out
100	// to every bound peer (see attachSharedPathLocked / RemovePath).
101	type sharedPathState struct {
102		name string
103		id   uint8
104		src  netip.Addr
105		conn *net.UDPConn
106	
107		// views is the per-peer VIEW set of THIS shared socket — one peerPathState per bound
108		// peer — published copy-on-write through an atomic.Pointer so the single Bind-owned
109		// readLoop demuxes an inbound datagram to its owning peer's view WITHOUT m.mu on the
110		// receive hot path (the same lock-free-publish discipline peersView/resequencer use).
111		// It is (re)published under m.mu at every fan-out site (Open / attachPeerPathLocked /
112		// deferred promote, and each concentrator peer bind). On the single-peer edge/hub it
113		// holds exactly one entry (the primary's view), which handleInbound reads as "no demux
114		// needed" — the byte-identical fast path. len(views)>1 marks a shared concentrator
115		// socket whose datagrams must be source-demuxed to the owning peer (T88).
116		views atomic.Pointer[[]*peerPathState]
117	}
118	
119	// addViewLocked publishes pp as a per-peer view of this shared socket for the lock-free
120	// receive demux (T88). Copy-on-write: it republishes a NEW slice with pp appended, so a
121	// readLoop mid-iteration over the previously-published snapshot is never disturbed. The
122	// caller holds m.mu (every fan-out site does), which serializes writers; readers Load
123	// without a lock.
124	func (sp *sharedPathState) addViewLocked(pp *peerPathState) {
125		old := sp.views.Load()
126		var next []*peerPathState
127		if old != nil {
128			next = make([]*peerPathState, len(*old), len(*old)+1)
129			copy(next, *old)
130		}
131		next = append(next, pp)
132		sp.views.Store(&next)
133	}
134	
135	// peerPathState is one peer's per-(peer,path) VIEW of a shared uplink: that peer's own
136	// decode Codec (each path receives on its own goroutine, so the Codec's scratch is never
137	// shared), the peer's own learned/configured return remote, the peer's own probe
138	// initiator for this path, and the peer's own per-path OUTER-wire byte counters. The
139	// remote is either configured (edge dest_addr / peer endpoint) or LEARNED from inbound
140	// traffic (concentrator) — but it lives strictly BELOW the engine's single virtual
141	// endpoint, so the engine never sees this per-(peer,path) bookkeeping churn.
142	//
143	// It EMBEDS its *sharedPathState so the socket identity (name/id/src/conn) is reached
144	// transparently, and back-references the owning peerState so a receive handler resolves
145	// exactly that peer's resequencer/reflector/FEC decoder.
146	type peerPathState struct {
147		*sharedPathState
148		peer  *peerState
149		codec *frame.Codec
150		// prober is this (peer,path)'s own probe initiator (nil when the bind runs without the
151		// probe transport). It is set at path creation and immutable for the path's life,
152		// so the Bind-owned receive goroutine reaches it via the peerPathState it already
153		// holds — NOT through a shared, dynamically-mutated probers slice — which is
154		// what keeps echo handling race-free while paths are added/removed at runtime (T30).
155		prober *telemetry.Prober
156	
157		// txBytes/rxBytes are cumulative OUTER-wire byte counters for this (peer,path), the
158		// per-path traffic accounting the /metrics exposition reports (T23). txBytes counts
159		// the DATA-frame wire bytes this path egresses on the Send hot path; rxBytes counts
160		// every outer datagram this path's readLoop receives (DATA, PROBE, and echo alike —
161		// the true received wire volume). They are atomics so the send/receive hot paths
162		// increment them WITHOUT taking m.mu (lock-free), and the scrape/snapshot path reads
163		// them with a plain atomic Load; a single writer per counter (Send holds m.mu while
164		// choosing the path, and each path has exactly one readLoop) means the Add is
165		// uncontended in the common case. "Bytes on Starlink vs 5G" — the data-thrift signal
166		// (requirement 2) — is exactly txBytes: when the weighted scheduler collapses to the
167		// primary under sub-capacity load, the backup path's Send count stays ~flat.
168		txBytes atomic.Uint64
169		rxBytes atomic.Uint64
170	
171		mu        sync.Mutex
172		remote    netip.AddrPort
173		hasRemote bool
174	}
175	
176	// peerState holds the per-PEER datapath state that was a process-global singleton before
177	// the multi-peer concentrator split: the SINGLE virtual endpoint the engine holds for this
178	// peer, the peer's send scheduler, its own outer-seq space, its shared send Codec, its
179	// probe reflector, its receive resequencer and FEC send/receive planes, its per-path probe
180	// initiators, and its per-(peer,path) views over the shared sockets. The single-peer
181	// edge/hub constructs EXACTLY ONE peerState (so behaviour is byte-identical to the pre-split
182	// singleton — Multipath embeds it as the primary and the datapath reaches its fields through
183	// that embed); the concentrator constructs one peerState per bound peer. resequencer and
184	// fecRecv stay atomic.Pointer, and outerSeq atomic, so the lock-free receive/send fast paths
185	// read them WITHOUT m.mu.
186	type peerState struct {
187		// name is the peer id/name, the key under which Multipath.peersByName holds this peer.
188		// Empty on the single-peer edge/hub (there is only one peer to key).
189		name string
190	
191		// psk is THIS peer's effective pre-shared key: the sole seam from which every frame
192		// Codec this peer derives (its sendCodec and each per-(peer,path) receive codec, via
193		// newCodec) and its probe Reflector authenticate. The single-peer edge/hub sets it to
194		// the one configured psk (so behaviour is byte-identical to the pre-split singleton);
195		// the concentrator sets a DIFFERENT psk per bound peer, so one peer's codec/reflector
196		// rejects another peer's frames (T84). NewCodec/NewReflector PSK-derivation itself is
197		// unchanged — only WHICH psk each peer feeds them differs.
198		psk config.Key
199	
200		// Immutable per-peer collaborators, built at construction and persisting across the
201		// Open→Close socket lifecycle (the concentrator pins virt's destination once for the
202		// process life, so virt in particular must NOT be recreated per Open).
203		virt      *udpEndpoint
204		scheduler sched.Scheduler
205		reflector *telemetry.Reflector
206		// newProber mints a prober for a path admitted to THIS peer at runtime (T30); nil
207		// disables runtime path addition for the peer (a bind without the probe transport).
208		newProber ProberFactory
209		// probers is this peer's BOOT-TIME per-path probe initiator set, in durable-membership
210		// (m.defs) order — bound AND deferred — shared with this peer's scheduler. At Open each
211		// bound entry is bound onto its peerPathState (pp.prober), and thereafter the hot paths
212		// reach a path's prober through the peerPathState — never by indexing this slice — so a
213		// runtime path add/remove cannot race echo handling. Nil when the peer runs without the
214		// probe transport; a nil here also gates the whole probe loop off.
215		probers []*telemetry.Prober
216	
217		// Per-Open state, (re)built by Open and cleared by Close.
218		sendCodec *frame.Codec
219		paths     []*peerPathState
220		outerSeq  atomic.Uint64
221		// resequencer is this peer's shared T18 receive resequencing buffer. Published
222		// atomically so the per-path readLoop goroutines read it WITHOUT m.mu.
223		resequencer atomic.Pointer[reseq.Resequencer]
224		// FEC datapath (T24), per peer. Both published atomically like resequencer so the
225		// lock-free receive fast path Loads fecRecv, and the lazy re-instantiation on re-bind
226		// (ensurePeerReceiveInstantiated, on a readLoop that must not take m.mu) can Store
227		// fecSend without racing Send's under-m.mu Load. The ENCODER inside fecSend is still
228		// mutated (Admit/Tick) only under m.mu — the atomic pointer governs publication of the
229		// fecSender object, not its encoder's single-writer discipline. Both nil when FEC is off.
230		fecSend atomic.Pointer[fecSender]
231		fecRecv atomic.Pointer[fecReceiver]
232	
233		// lifecycleMu serializes lazy (re)instantiation of the heavy trio (resequencer, fecRecv,
234		// fecSend) on a readLoop goroutine (ensurePeerReceiveInstantiated) against teardown's
235		// clearing of that trio (teardownPeerLocked), so a teardown interleaving mid-instantiation
236		// can never leave a half-published plane (a fecRecv/fecSend without its resequencer) nor
237		// resurrect a plane on a torn-down peer that the next re-bind then reuses stale. It is a
238		// LEAF lock taken either ALONE (instantiation — which must never take m.mu, so Close's
239		// readersWG.Wait under m.mu cannot deadlock on it) or UNDER m.mu (teardown), giving the
240		// single fixed order m.mu -> lifecycleMu and no cycle (instantiation never reaches for m.mu).
241		lifecycleMu sync.Mutex
242	}
243	
244	// newPeerState builds the durable per-peer datapath state whose probe Reflector — and,
245	// through ps.psk, every frame Codec this peer later derives in Open/AddPath (its sendCodec
246	// and each per-(peer,path) receive codec, via newCodec) — authenticate under THIS peer's
247	// psk. It is the seam that replaces the pre-split single-psk assumption (T84): the
248	// single-peer edge/hub mints exactly one (the primary) from the sole configured psk, while
249	// the concentrator mints one per bound peer from that peer's DIFFERENT effective psk, so a
250	// peerState built from a different psk gets its own codec/reflector and cross-psk frames are
251	// rejected. virt is created here (not per Open) because the concentrator pins its destination
252	// once for the process life. The Reflector draws its per-path challenges from crypto/rand,
253	// exactly as before. The per-Open fields (sendCodec, paths, resequencer, FEC planes) are
254	// left zero for Open to (re)build.
255	func newPeerState(name string, psk config.Key, scheduler sched.Scheduler, newProber ProberFactory, probers []*telemetry.Prober) *peerState {
256		return &peerState{
257			name:      name,
258			psk:       psk,
259			virt:      &udpEndpoint{},
260			scheduler: scheduler,
261			reflector: telemetry.NewReflector(psk, rand.Reader),
262			newProber: newProber,
263			probers:   probers,
264		}
265	}
266	
267	// newCodec derives a fresh frame Codec bound to THIS peer's psk — the send Codec at Open and
268	// each per-(peer,path) receive Codec share the derivation but not the instance, since a Codec
269	// is not safe for concurrent use (each receive path decodes on its own goroutine). Deriving
270	// from ps.psk (not a Multipath-wide key) is what makes a path's receive Codec the codec of the
271	// peer the path is bound to (T84).
272	func (ps *peerState) newCodec() (*frame.Codec, error) {
273		return frame.NewCodec(ps.psk)
274	}
275	
276	// deferredPath is a configured path whose WELL-FORMED source_addr was not yet
277	// assignable at Open (net.ListenUDP -> EADDRNOTAVAIL: no interface holds the
278	// address, e.g. a 5G modem with no DHCP lease at boot). Rather than tear the whole
279	// bond down, Open records the path here instead of binding it: the tunnel comes up
280	// on the paths that DID bind, and this path stays DOWN — its prober, never fed an
281	// echo, reports StateDown, which the scheduler excludes from Pick, exactly as the
282	// runtime path-down model treats a live-but-silent path. It carries the boot-time
283	// prober so the T55 background reconcile that retries the bind reuses the SAME
284	// path-id stamp instead of minting a new one. prober is nil only on a bind without
285	// the probe transport, which never defers (see Open).
286	type deferredPath struct {
287		def    config.Path
288		prober *telemetry.Prober
289	}
290	
291	func (ps *peerPathState) setRemote(ap netip.AddrPort) {
292		ps.mu.Lock()
293		ps.remote, ps.hasRemote = ap, true
294		ps.mu.Unlock()
295	}
296	
297	func (ps *peerPathState) getRemote() (netip.AddrPort, bool) {
298		ps.mu.Lock()
299		defer ps.mu.Unlock()
300		return ps.remote, ps.hasRemote
301	}
302	
303	// Multipath is the P1 bonding conn.Bind: one UDP socket per configured path
304	// (bound to the path's source address), all fronted by a SINGLE stable virtual
305	// endpoint the engine holds per peer. Send wraps each opaque WireGuard datagram
306	// in an outer DATA frame (own outer-seq + path-id) and picks a path; one
307	// Bind-owned reader per path unwraps DATA frames into the shared resequencer, and
308	// a SINGLE engine-facing ReceiveFunc drains it and hands the inner WG datagram up
309	// under the shared virtual endpoint (the fan-in that lets paths be added/removed at
310	// runtime without the engine spawning receive goroutines — T30). It replaces
311	// bind.Passthrough behind the same conn.Bind seam (device wiring is unchanged).
312	//
313	// Lifecycle: the sockets live for the duration of an Open→Close span, NOT the
314	// Multipath's whole life. The amneziawg engine calls Close() before every Open()
315	// (device.upLocked → BindUpdate → closeBindLocked, and on IpcSet listen_port /
316	// route-change events) and cycles Close↔Open on Down/Up, so — exactly like
317	// conn.StdNetBind — Open creates the per-path sockets and Close tears them down
318	// AND clears the path state so the next Open rebuilds from scratch. The "closed"
319	// state is simply "no bound sockets" (len(paths)==0); there is no separate sticky
320	// flag that a later Open would have to reset.
321	//
322	// Path policy: an injected sched.Scheduler (T15) chooses the egress path per
323	// datagram. The P1 MVP wires an active-backup scheduler (all traffic on the
324	// preferred primary, transparent failover to a backup on a T13 path-DOWN signal
325	// with failback hysteresis); the weighted/FEC-aware policy (T21) is a different
326	// Scheduler swapped in here with no Bind change. No FEC/resequencing yet
327	// (P2/P3), but every DATA frame carries its outer-seq and path-id so T18/T24 can
328	// consume them.
329	// ProberFactory mints a *telemetry.Prober for a path admitted at runtime (T30),
330	// stamped with the given stable path-id. The device wires it capturing the SAME
331	// per-boot session id, prober config, clock, PSK, and logger the boot-time probers
332	// were built with, so a runtime path's liveness is measured identically to a
333	// boot-time path (its probes join the same session so the peer's reflector adopts
334	// them without a challenge reset). It is nil on a bind built without the probe
335	// transport (the T12 unit tests), which therefore cannot add paths at runtime.
336	type ProberFactory func(name string, id uint8) *telemetry.Prober
337	
338	type Multipath struct {
339		defs []config.Path
340	
341		// classify maps each outbound datagram to its pacer traffic class from the inner
342		// WireGuard message type, parameterized by the tunnel's Amnezia obfuscation profile
343		// so a WireGuard control frame (handshake/keepalive) is recognised — and pacing-
344		// exempted — under advanced security too, not only in vanilla mode (defect D22). It
345		// is immutable after construction and holds no lock, so Send reads it off m.mu.
346		classify wgClassifier
347	
348		// deferredListen binds a reconciled deferred path's socket (T55 background
349		// reconcile). It is an injection seam: the default pins the source IP unless dev is
350		// set (net.ListenUDP / listenPath, matching AddPath's runtime bind — I5), and a test
351		// overrides it to drive the deferred→bound transition deterministically — a
352		// source_addr "becoming assignable" — without a real interface address having to
353		// appear on the host. dev is resolveForcedDeviceBind's decision for the deferred
354		// path's resolved BindMode, computed by reconcileDeferred before the call. Immutable
355		// after construction, never nil.
356		deferredListen func(src netip.Addr, port uint16, dev string) (*net.UDPConn, error)
357	
358		// resolveDeviceBind decides AddPath's and reconcileDeferred's per-path forced-
359		// device bind (I5): whether a path's RESOLVED BindMode is config.BindModeDevice,
360		// and if so, the interface its source_addr currently resolves to (see
361		// resolveForcedDeviceBind — it has no other-path contention to check, unlike
362		// Open's planPathBinds/selectDeviceBinds). It is an injection seam mirroring
363		// deferredListen: the default is the real resolveForcedDeviceBind (a
364		// net.Interfaces() snapshot), and a test overrides it to drive a BindModeDevice
365		// path's dev deterministically without a real interface having to appear on the
366		// host (T106 round 2). Immutable after construction, never nil.
367		resolveDeviceBind func(src netip.Addr, mode config.BindMode) string
368	
369		// addPathListen binds AddPath's runtime-admitted path's socket. It is an
370		// injection seam mirroring deferredListen, scoped to AddPath's OWN call site:
371		// AddPath calls the package-level listenPath directly (it is not routed through
372		// deferredListen, which only reconcileDeferred drives), so without this seam no
373		// test can observe what dev AddPath actually threads into the bind — resolving a
374		// BindModeDevice interface via resolveDeviceBind and then discarding it at the
375		// listen call passes the full suite (T106 round 3). The default is the real
376		// listenPath; a test overrides it to capture the dev argument deterministically
377		// without a real interface having to appear on the host. Immutable after
378		// construction, never nil.
379		addPathListen func(src netip.Addr, port uint16, dev string) (*net.UDPConn, error)
380	
381		mu sync.Mutex
382	
383		// The PRIMARY peer, embedded so the single-peer datapath (Send, the receive drainer,
384		// the probe loop) and the existing single-peer tests reach its fields — virt,
385		// scheduler, reflector, newProber, probers, sendCodec, paths, outerSeq, resequencer,
386		// fecSend/fecRecv — transparently through promotion, keeping behaviour byte-identical
387		// to the pre-split singleton. It is peers[0]; the concentrator's additional peers live
388		// in peers/peersByName. The former process-global resequencer/outerSeq/scheduler now
389		// live on this peerState, NOT on Multipath.
390		*peerState
391	
392		// peers is every bound peer (len 1 on the single-peer edge/hub). The runtime shared-
393		// path add/remove fan-out iterates it so per-(peer,path) state is created/torn-down for
394		// EVERY currently-bound peer (attachSharedPathLocked / RemovePath). peersByName keys
395		// them by peer id/name.
396		peers       []*peerState
397		peersByName map[string]*peerState
398		// peersView is the LOCK-FREE snapshot of the bound peer set the single engine-facing
399		// receive drainer iterates (newReceiveFunc). m.peers is mutated only under m.mu (peer
400		// wiring / fan-out); every mutation republishes this pointer (republishPeersLocked) so
401		// the drainer enumerates EVERY bound peer's resequencer WITHOUT taking m.mu on the
402		// receive hot path — the same lock-free-publish discipline resequencer/fecRecv use. It
403		// is never nil after construction (the constructor publishes the primary-only view).
404		peersView atomic.Pointer[[]*peerState]
405		// peerByEndpoint is the inbound endpoint-keyed demux placeholder (still unused today: the
406		// single-peer edge/hub needs no demux, and the source-keyed binding below is what the
407		// concentrator receive path routes on).
408		peerByEndpoint map[netip.AddrPort]*peerState
409		// peerBySource is the inbound source-demux map: a learned source address → the peer it was
410		// bound to by an authenticated PROBE (T88). The concentrator's per-socket readLoops resolve
411		// a datagram's source to its owning peer through it; a source is bound ONLY on the first
412		// PROBE that MAC-verifies under a peer's psk (D9/D11: bindings, like remotes, are learned
413		// only from authenticated PROBEs). It is published copy-on-write through an atomic.Pointer
414		// so the receive hot path resolves a bound source with a lock-free Load (no m.mu — a reader
415		// must never block on m.mu, since Close waits on the readers WHILE holding it), and a new
416		// binding is installed lock-free by a CAS republish of a copy with the entry added
417		// (bindSourceToPeer). Nil until the first binding; the single-peer edge/hub never consults
418		// it (one peer owns every socket — handleInbound's fast path skips the demux entirely).
419		peerBySource atomic.Pointer[map[netip.Addr]*peerState]
420		// maxDemuxSources caps peerBySource (the provisional/unbound-source demux state) so a
421		// bootstrap flood cannot grow it without bound (Q26/Q27, see defaultMaxDemuxSources). Set
422		// once at construction and read on the lock-free bind path (bindSourceToPeer); a test may
423		// lower it to exercise cap-exhaustion. Zero (never set) means "no cap".
424		maxDemuxSources int
425		// peerByVirt routes an OUTBOUND Send to its owning peer: the engine hands Send the
426		// single virtual endpoint (*udpEndpoint) it holds for a peer, and this map resolves
427		// that pointer to the peer's datapath state (outerSeq, scheduler, sendCodec, fecSend,
428		// per-(peer,path) set). It is the SEND-side dual of peerByEndpoint/peerBySource (which
429		// demux INBOUND datagrams). Each peer's virt is DISTINCT, so the lookup is exact; an
430		// endpoint not in this map is an unknown peer, which Send refuses rather than
431		// misrouting onto some other peer's paths. The primary is registered at construction;
432		// the concentrator registers each additional peer as it binds it. Read under m.mu on
433		// the Send path; written under m.mu (or at single-threaded construction).
434		peerByVirt map[*udpEndpoint]*peerState
435	
436		// shared is the SHARED per-socket path list (the sockets themselves), rebuilt from
437		// m.defs on every Open and mutated by the runtime add/remove. Each bound peer holds a
438		// peerPathState VIEW over a subset of these; on the single-peer edge/hub primary.paths
439		// is index-aligned with shared. len(shared)==0 is the "closed" (no sockets) state.
440		shared []*sharedPathState
441	
442		// deferred holds the configured paths whose well-formed source_addr was not yet
443		// assignable at the last Open (EADDRNOTAVAIL). They are NOT in shared or the
444		// scheduler — the tunnel runs on the paths that bound — but are recorded here,
445		// index-independent of shared, for the T55 background reconcile to retry as their
446		// addresses appear. Rebuilt from scratch on every Open; guarded by m.mu. The deferred-
447		// path machinery is SHARED (per-socket): a promoted deferred path fans its per-(peer,
448		// path) state out to every peer exactly as a runtime AddPath does.
449		deferred []deferredPath
450		// defaultRemote is the fallback per-path remote (the peer's wireguard
451		// endpoint) applied to any path without its own dest_addr. It may be set by
452		// ParseEndpoint BEFORE Open, so it is stored here and applied at Open time.
453		defaultRemote    netip.AddrPort
454		hasDefaultRemote bool
455	
456		// fecCfg / adaptiveCfg are the FEC configuration (T24/T29), shared by every peer; the
457		// per-peer fecSend/fecRecv runtime state Open builds from them lives on peerState. nil
458		// when FEC (respectively adaptive FEC) is disabled — the datapath is then byte-for-byte
459		// the pre-T24 behaviour. See fec.go.
460		fecCfg      *fec.Config
461		adaptiveCfg *adaptivefec.Config
462	
463		// Receive fan-in (T30). To let a path be added at runtime WITHOUT the engine
464		// spawning a new receive goroutine (it only builds its receive goroutines once,
465		// from the ReceiveFuncs Open returns), the per-path socket reads are decoupled
466		// from the engine-facing delivery: the Bind owns one readLoop goroutine PER path
467		// (tracked by readersWG) that reads its socket and feeds the shared resequencer,
468		// and Open returns a SINGLE engine-facing ReceiveFunc that drains the resequencer
469		// in order. A reader pokes deliverSignal after each datagram so the drainer wakes;
470		// recvClosed is closed by Close to release both the drainer and any reader parked
471		// on a delivery. openPort mirrors Open's bind port so a runtime-added path binds
472		// consistently; nextPathID is a monotonic high-water that persists ACROSS Open
473		// spans (a Close->Open never lowers it; only a process restart resets it), so a
474		// surviving path is never renumbered and a freed id is never reused for the
475		// process lifetime, and thus can never collide with the peer's per-path reflector
476		// state.
477		deliverSignal chan struct{}
478		recvClosed    chan struct{}
479		readersWG     sync.WaitGroup
480		openPort      uint16
481		nextPathID    uint16
482	
483		// Receive-path liveness sweep (T39, defect D15). Liveness DOWN-detection normally
484		// rides StartProbeLoop's single wall-clock ticker goroutine (emitProbes → Tick).
485		// Under heavy CPU load — e.g. the concentrator absorbing a saturating forward flood
486		// on 4 vCPU — that timer goroutine can be scheduled with ~1s jitter, delaying a
487		// path-DOWN transition (and thus the reply-direction failover) past the P1 budget.
488		// The per-path receive goroutines, in contrast, are the goroutines the inbound
489		// traffic is ALREADY scheduling, so driving Tick from them re-evaluates liveness
490		// even when the timer goroutine is starved. sweepIntervalNanos is the probe
491		// interval (0 until StartProbeLoop arms it — a bind without the probe transport
492		// never sweeps); lastSweepNanos is the wall-clock high-water throttling the sweep
493		// to at most once per interval so the receive hot path stays cheap.
494		sweepIntervalNanos atomic.Int64
495		lastSweepNanos     atomic.Int64
496	
497		// everUp is the STICKY "ever had a live path" predicate (I4): set true the first time
498		// ANY path, for ANY bound peer, reaches liveness telemetry.StateUp (dispatchInbound,
499		// on a fresh probe echo), and never cleared afterward — a later total outage is a
500		// genuine failure signal, not a startup warmup. The device-package engineLogger
501		// adapter consults EverHadLivePath to downgrade the startup no-healthy-path spam
502		// (ErrNoHealthyPath) to a single coalesced INFO line until the first path comes up,
503		// then lets it log at ERROR (a real outage) from then on.
504		everUp atomic.Bool
505	}
506	
507	// compile-time proof that Multipath satisfies the engine's Bind contract.
508	var _ Bind = (*Multipath)(nil)
509	
510	// NewMultipath returns a closed multipath Bind over the configured paths; call
511	// Open to bind the per-path sockets. The PSK keys the outer framing and must be
512	// set (config validation guarantees it). The scheduler is the injected send-side
513	// path-selection policy (T15) whose priority order MUST match the paths slice
514	// order (index 0 = the preferred primary); it is a required collaborator so the
515	// send path is never without a policy.
516	//
517	// probers is the per-path probe initiator that drives on-wire liveness (T13/T37).
518	// When non-nil it MUST hold exactly one *telemetry.Prober per path, in path order,
519	// and those SAME values MUST be the scheduler's PathHealth sources so the liveness
520	// the probe loop measures is the liveness the scheduler selects on. Pass nil to
521	// run the bind without the probe transport (the T12 unit tests drive selection via
522	// sched.AlwaysUp instead). A Reflector is built from the PSK to answer peer probes.
523	//
524	// newProber is the factory the runtime path-add path (AddPath, T30) uses to mint a
525	// prober for a newly-admitted path; it must be non-nil to allow AddPath and MUST be
526	// paired with a non-nil probers (the boot-time set) — a factory without a boot-time
527	// slice would let AddPath append to a nil m.probers, breaking the m.paths/m.probers
528	// alignment invariant and panicking on the next Open at m.probers[i]. Pass nil to
529	// forbid runtime path addition.
530	//
531	// fecCfg is the fixed-ratio Reed-Solomon FEC configuration (T24). Pass nil to run the
532	// datapath with FEC disabled (the pre-T24 behaviour, byte-for-byte); a non-nil,
533	// pre-validated config turns the send-side parity encoder and receive-side recovery
534	// decoder on. It is validated here (fail fast) so an invalid ratio is rejected at
535	// construction rather than at the first Open.
536	// adaptiveCfg is the adaptive-FEC controller configuration (T29). Pass nil for the
537	// fixed-ratio behaviour (T24); a non-nil config REQUIRES a non-nil fecCfg (the
538	// controller resizes the FEC encoder's parity — it is meaningless with the plane off)
539	// and reinterprets fecCfg.ParityShards as the controller's parity ceiling. It is
540	// validated here (fail fast) so a mis-tuned control law is rejected at construction.
541	//
542	// amnezia is the tunnel's AmneziaWG obfuscation profile (config.Amnezia). It
543	// parameterizes the send-path frame-type classifier so a WireGuard control frame is
544	// recognised and pacing-exempted under advanced security — custom magic headers and
545	// handshake junk prefixes — as well as in vanilla mode (defect D22). Pass the zero value
546	// for a vanilla (unobfuscated) tunnel; the classifier then uses the default type words
547	// and no junk prefix. It does NOT need to match the config's validated state — an
548	// all-zero profile is exactly the vanilla classifier.
549	func NewMultipath(paths []config.Path, psk config.Key, scheduler sched.Scheduler, probers []*telemetry.Prober, newProber ProberFactory, fecCfg *fec.Config, adaptiveCfg *adaptivefec.Config, amnezia config.Amnezia) (*Multipath, error) {
550		if len(paths) == 0 {
551			return nil, errors.New("bind: at least one path is required")
552		}
553		if !psk.IsSet() {
554			return nil, errors.New("bind: PSK is required for outer framing")
555		}
556		if len(paths) > 256 {
557			// path-id is a uint8 in the DATA frame header.
558			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bind: at most 256 paths supported, got %d", len(paths))
559		}
560		if scheduler == nil {
561			return nil, errors.New("bind: a send scheduler is required")
562		}
563		if newProber != nil && probers == nil {
564			// A runtime-path factory without a boot-time prober slice would let AddPath append
565			// to a nil m.probers, desyncing m.paths from m.probers and panicking on the next
566			// Open at m.probers[i]. The two are paired by construction; enforce it here.
567			return nil, errors.New("bind: newProber requires a non-nil probers (boot-time set)")
568		}
569		if probers != nil && len(probers) != len(paths) {
570			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bind: probers must have one entry per path (got %d, want %d)", len(probers), len(paths))
571		}
572		for i, pr := range probers {
573			if pr == nil {
574				return nil, fmt.Errorf("bind: prober %d is nil", i)
575			}
576		}
577		if fecCfg != nil {
578			if err := fecCfg.Validate(); err != nil {
579				return nil, fmt.Errorf("bind: invalid FEC configuration: %w", err)
580			}
581			if fecCfg.Deadline > maxFECDeadline {
582				// A deadline this large makes every deadline-flushed group's recovery
583				// structurally late: the resequencer skips the gap (resequencerTimeout) before
584				// the parity-derived frames land, so recovery never delivers. Reject it rather
585				// than ship a bond whose FEC silently cannot help (defect #4).
586				return nil, fmt.Errorf("bind: fec deadline %s exceeds the max %s (must stay safely below the resequencer's %s per-gap timeout so deadline-flushed recovery lands before the gap is skipped)", fecCfg.Deadline, maxFECDeadline, resequencerTimeout)
587			}
588		}
589		if adaptiveCfg != nil {
590			if fecCfg == nil {
591				return nil, errors.New("bind: adaptive FEC requires a FEC configuration (the controller resizes the FEC encoder's parity)")
592			}
593			if err := adaptiveCfg.Validate(); err != nil {
594				return nil, fmt.Errorf("bind: invalid adaptive FEC configuration: %w", err)
595			}
596			// The controller's parity ceiling (MaxParity) is the fixed FEC ParityShards both
597			// ends agree on: the receiver's decoder is built at fecCfg.ParityShards, and the
598			// encoder must never emit more parity than that ceiling (SetParity clamps to it).
599			// A mismatch would let the controller target a parity index the decoder rejects,
600			// so bind the two together at construction rather than trusting the caller.
601			if adaptiveCfg.MaxParity != fecCfg.ParityShards {
602				return nil, fmt.Errorf("bind: adaptive FEC parity ceiling (MaxParity=%d) must equal the FEC parity_shards (%d), which the receiver's decoder is built at", adaptiveCfg.MaxParity, fecCfg.ParityShards)
603			}
604			if adaptiveCfg.DataShards != fecCfg.DataShards {
605				return nil, fmt.Errorf("bind: adaptive FEC DataShards (%d) must equal the FEC data_shards (%d)", adaptiveCfg.DataShards, fecCfg.DataShards)
606			}
607		}
608		// The single-peer edge/hub constructs EXACTLY ONE peerState (the primary). virt is
609		// created here — NOT per Open — because the concentrator pins its destination once for
610		// the process life and every existing test relies on the virtual-endpoint pointer being
611		// stable across Open/Close/add/remove.
612		primary := newPeerState("", psk, scheduler, newProber, probers)
613		m := &Multipath{
614			defs:              append([]config.Path(nil), paths...),
615			classify:          newWGClassifier(amnezia),
616			deferredListen:    defaultDeferredListen,
617			resolveDeviceBind: resolveForcedDeviceBind,
618			addPathListen:     listenPath,
619			peerState:         primary,
620			peers:             []*peerState{primary},
621			peersByName:       map[string]*peerState{primary.name: primary},
622			peerByEndpoint:    map[netip.AddrPort]*peerState{},
623			peerByVirt:        map[*udpEndpoint]*peerState{primary.virt: primary},
624			maxDemuxSources:   defaultMaxDemuxSources,
625			fecCfg:            fecCfg,
626			adaptiveCfg:       adaptiveCfg,
627		}
628		// Publish the initial (primary-only) peer view the receive drainer iterates. No
629		// concurrency yet — the bind is not open — so this runs without m.mu.
630		m.republishPeersLocked()
631		return m, nil
632	}
633	
634	// republishPeersLocked snapshots m.peers into the lock-free peersView the engine-facing
635	// receive drainer (newReceiveFunc) iterates. The caller MUST hold m.mu whenever the bind
636	// can be open (so the snapshot never races a concurrent m.peers append); the constructor
637	// is the sole exception (it runs before the Multipath is reachable by any goroutine). The
638	// snapshot is a fresh slice so a later append to m.peers never mutates a view the drainer
639	// is mid-iteration over.
640	func (m *Multipath) republishPeersLocked() {
641		snap := make([]*peerState, len(m.peers))
642		copy(snap, m.peers)
643		m.peersView.Store(&snap)
644	}
645	
646	// AddConcentratorPeer registers one ADDITIONAL bound peer with the Bind — the concentrator's
647	// per-peer wiring (G4/T93). Peer 0 (the embedded primary) is built by NewMultipath; the
648	// concentrator calls this once per additional configured peer, each with its OWN effective psk,
649	// send scheduler, boot-time per-path prober set, and runtime prober factory (all keyed on that
650	// peer's psk, so one peer's codec/reflector reject another's frames — T84/R72). The peer's
651	// STABLE virtual endpoint is minted here (newPeerState) and registered in peerByVirt so an
652	// outbound Send routes replies back to THIS peer; Open then builds this peer's per-(peer,path)
653	// view of every bound socket, reconciles its scheduler, and (via newReceiveFunc) reports its
654	// virt to the engine on the first inbound frame (invariant A1: one virtual endpoint per peer).
655	//
656	// It MUST be called BEFORE Open (while the bind is closed): the per-(peer,path) views are
657	// rebuilt by Open from the registered peer set on every Open span (including each Close→Open
658	// cycle the engine drives on Down/Up and route changes), so a peer registered after the sockets
659	// are bound would be view-less and its DATA/PROBE never routed. probers, when supplied, must be
660	// index-aligned with the configured path membership (m.defs), exactly as the primary's are.
661	func (m *Multipath) AddConcentratorPeer(name string, psk config.Key, scheduler sched.Scheduler, probers []*telemetry.Prober, newProber ProberFactory) error {
662		if name == "" {
663			return errors.New("bind: concentrator peer name is required")
664		}
665		if !psk.IsSet() {
666			return errors.New("bind: concentrator peer psk is required")
667		}
668		if scheduler == nil {
669			return errors.New("bind: concentrator peer requires a send scheduler")
670		}
671		if newProber != nil && probers == nil {
672			return errors.New("bind: newProber requires a non-nil probers (boot-time set)")
673		}
674		m.mu.Lock()
675		defer m.mu.Unlock()
676		if len(m.paths) != 0 {
677			return errors.New("bind: concentrator peers must be registered before Open")
678		}
679		if probers != nil && len(probers) != len(m.defs) {
680			return fmt.Errorf("bind: concentrator peer %q: probers must have one entry per configured path (got %d, want %d)", name, len(probers), len(m.defs))
681		}
682		for i, pr := range probers {
683			if pr == nil {
684				return fmt.Errorf("bind: concentrator peer %q: prober %d is nil", name, i)
685			}
686		}
687		if name == m.name {
688			return fmt.Errorf("bind: concentrator peer name %q collides with the primary peer", name)
689		}
690		if _, dup := m.peersByName[name]; dup {
691			return fmt.Errorf("bind: duplicate concentrator peer name %q", name)
692		}
693		p := newPeerState(name, psk, scheduler, newProber, probers)
694		m.peers = append(m.peers, p)
695		m.peersByName[name] = p
696		m.peerByVirt[p.virt] = p
697		// Publish the grown peer set so the engine-facing receive drainer (newReceiveFunc) will
698		// enumerate this peer's resequencer once Open builds it.
699		m.republishPeersLocked()
700		return nil
701	}
702	
703	// Open binds one UDP socket per configured path to that path's source address on
704	// port (0 = random per socket), sets a large SO_RCVBUF on each, spawns one
705	// Bind-owned reader per path, and returns a SINGLE engine-facing ReceiveFunc (the
706	// fan-in drainer) plus the first path's bound port. Each path whose config carries
707	// a dest_addr — or, failing that, the peer's wireguard endpoint learned via
708	// ParseEndpoint — starts with a known remote; the rest are learned from inbound
709	// traffic.
710	//
711	// Open is the sole creator of the per-path sockets: the engine's bring-up path
712	// calls Close() first (on the still-unopened bind) and then Open(), so building
713	// the sockets HERE — not in NewMultipath — is what makes that Close→Open sequence
714	// (and every subsequent Down→Up) work. The engine passes the previously-bound
715	// port back on a re-Open; each path binds to it on its own distinct source
716	// address, and the first path's bound port is returned so the engine keeps a
717	// stable listen port across the cycle (matching conn.StdNetBind).
718	func (m *Multipath) Open(port uint16) ([]ReceiveFunc, uint16, error) {
719		m.mu.Lock()
720		defer m.mu.Unlock()
721		if len(m.paths) != 0 {
722			return nil, 0, conn.ErrBindAlreadyOpen
723		}
724	
725		// (Re)build the per-Open datapath planes — send Codec, receive resequencer, and FEC
726		// send/receive state — fresh for this bring-up, for EVERY bound peer (not just the
727		// primary via promotion). This keeps Open symmetric with closeSocketsLocked, which
728		// clears every peer's per-Open state: a concentrator peer bound before Open must get
729		// its OWN fresh planes on each Close→Open cycle, and one peer's (re)creation must never
730		// touch another peer's release point or FEC group state. On the single-peer edge/hub
731		// m.peers holds only the primary, so this is byte-identical to the pre-split rebuild.
732		// See openPeerDatapathLocked.
733		for _, p := range m.peers {
734			if err := m.openPeerDatapathLocked(p); err != nil {
735				_ = m.closeSocketsLocked()
736				return nil, 0, err
737			}
738		}
739	
740		// Resolve every path's interface up front and decide, per path, whether its
741		// socket may be device-bound (SO_BINDTODEVICE + wildcard — survives a T16
742		// re-roam) or must pin the specific source IP (the pre-T16 behaviour, required
743		// when paths share an interface or the interface is multi-address, so distinct
744		// specific-IP sockets coexist on one port without an EADDRINUSE collision). See
745		// selectDeviceBinds.
746		srcs := make([]netip.Addr, len(m.defs))
747		modes := make([]config.BindMode, len(m.defs))
748		for i := range m.defs {
749			srcs[i] = m.defs[i].SourceAddr
750			modes[i] = m.defs[i].Bind
751		}
752		bindDevs := planPathBinds(srcs, modes)
753	
754		// A path whose WELL-FORMED source_addr is merely NOT-YET-ASSIGNABLE at boot
755		// (EADDRNOTAVAIL: no interface holds the address — a 5G modem without a DHCP
756		// lease, Starlink mid-obstruction) is DEFERRED rather than treated as fatal, so
757		// the bond comes up on the paths that DO bind (G2/W1, approach A). Tolerating it
758		// requires the runtime path-down machinery: the deferred path's prober stays
759		// StateDown so the scheduler excludes it, and a DynamicScheduler whose membership
760		// can be reconciled to only the bound paths. Absent the probe transport (the T12
761		// no-prober unit binds) or a dynamic scheduler there is no such Down model, so —
762		// exactly as AddPath refuses a runtime add without probers — every bind error
763		// stays fatal there. A MALFORMED source_addr never reaches here (config.validate
764		// rejects it at load), and any OTHER bind error (EADDRINUSE, permission) is fatal.
765		_, dynOK := m.scheduler.(sched.DynamicScheduler)
766		tolerateDefer := m.probers != nil && dynOK
767		m.deferred = nil
768	
769		actualPort := port
770		firstBound := true
771		for i := range m.defs {
772			def := m.defs[i]
773			// Device-bind this path when selectDeviceBinds proved it safe (so a
774			// mid-session source-address change / T16 re-roam does not break the socket),
775			// otherwise pin the specific source IP. See selectDeviceBinds / listenPath.
776			c, err := listenPath(def.SourceAddr, port, bindDevs[i])
777			if err != nil {
778				if tolerateDefer && errors.Is(err, syscall.EADDRNOTAVAIL) {
779					// Defer this path: record its def + boot prober (kept Down) for the T55
780					// background reconcile to retry, and leave the bond to come up on the rest.
781					// Mirrors AddPath's rollback discipline — a failed path never disturbs the
782					// tunnel — at the boot boundary.
783					m.deferred = append(m.deferred, deferredPath{def: def, prober: m.probers[i]})
784					continue
785				}
786				_ = m.closeSocketsLocked()
787				return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("bind: open path %q on %s: %w", def.Name, def.SourceAddr, err)
788			}
789			// Large SO_RCVBUF, best-effort: the kernel caps at net.core.rmem_max and
790			// SetReadBuffer does not require privilege, so a returned error is rare;
791			// treat it as non-fatal rather than refusing to bind in a restricted env.
792			_ = c.SetReadBuffer(socketRecvBuffer)
793	
794			shared := &sharedPathState{name: def.Name, id: uint8(i), src: def.SourceAddr, conn: c}
795			// Build EVERY bound peer's view of this shared socket (T93): each peer decodes under its
796			// OWN psk-derived Codec and probes with its OWN per-(peer,path) prober, so a concentrator
797			// socket shared by several peers keeps each peer's authenticated stream isolated
798			// (demuxInbound source-demuxes on the first authenticated PROBE). The primary (peers[0])
799			// is built exactly as the pre-split single peer — byte-identical on the single-peer
800			// edge/hub, where m.peers holds only the primary. m.paths (the primary's path slice,
801			// reached through the embed) grows via the pi==0 append below; a concentrator peer's
802			// paths grow on its own peerState.
803			for pi, p := range m.peers {
804				// The path binds to peer p; its receive codec is p's codec (derived from p's psk).
805				codec, err := p.newCodec()
806				if err != nil {
807					_ = c.Close()
808					_ = m.closeSocketsLocked()
809					return nil, 0, err
810				}
811				pp := &peerPathState{sharedPathState: shared, peer: p, codec: codec}
812				if p.probers != nil {
813					// Fail fast rather than panic if a peer's prober slice ever falls short of the
814					// shared m.defs membership: every runtime admission (bound OR deferred) fans a
815					// per-peer prober out to EVERY peer (AddPath), so p.probers stays index-aligned
816					// with m.defs. A divergence is a wiring defect — surface it as a bind error
817					// instead of an index-out-of-range panic that would crash the daemon.
818					if i >= len(p.probers) {
819						_ = c.Close()
820						_ = m.closeSocketsLocked()
821						return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("bind: peer %q prober set (len %d) is shorter than the path membership at index %d — per-peer prober fan-out desync", p.name, len(p.probers), i)
822					}
823					pp.prober = p.probers[i]
824					if pi == 0 {
825						// Reconcile the SHARED DATA-frame path-id to the PRIMARY prober's IMMUTABLE
826						// stamp rather than the slice index i: after a runtime RemovePath the survivor
827						// keeps its original (higher) stamp, so index-based numbering would renumber a
828						// live path AND diverge its DATA id from its PROBE stamp. Every peer's
829						// probers[i] carries the SAME stamp (the device stamps each peer's boot prober
830						// for path i identically), so taking it from the primary is authoritative and
831						// keeps DATA and every peer's PROBE agreeing on the wire.
832						shared.id = pp.prober.PathID()
833					}
834				}
835				switch {
836				case def.DestAddr.IsValid():
837					pp.setRemote(def.DestAddr)
838				case m.hasDefaultRemote:
839					pp.setRemote(m.defaultRemote)
840				}
841				p.paths = append(p.paths, pp)
842				// Publish this peer's view for the receive demux (T88). A single-view socket is the
843				// edge/hub byte-identical fast path in demuxInbound; a socket with >1 view is
844				// source-demuxed to its owning peer on each authenticated PROBE.
845				shared.addViewLocked(pp)
846			}
847			m.shared = append(m.shared, shared)
848			if firstBound {
849				actualPort = uint16(c.LocalAddr().(*net.UDPAddr).Port)
850				firstBound = false
851			}
852		}
853	
854		// Hard guard (a): a bond with NO transport is impossible. If every configured path
855		// deferred, tolerance must NOT degrade to a zero-path bind — fail fatally, exactly
856		// as the pre-tolerance Open did when the sole path could not bind.
857		if len(m.paths) == 0 {
858			_ = m.closeSocketsLocked()
859			return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("bind: no configured path could bind its source address (all %d deferred as not-yet-assignable)", len(m.deferred))
860		}
861	
862		// nextPathID is a MONOTONIC HIGH-WATER carried ACROSS Open spans, never decreased.
863		// Close does not reset it, and here it is only ever RAISED to at least max(existing
864		// path stamp)+1. Resetting it to len(m.paths) (the pre-fix behaviour) re-minted an id
865		// a survivor still held once a RemovePath had opened a gap in the stamp space below
866		// that survivor's stamp: the next runtime AddPath then collided with the live path at
867		// an identical (PathID, SessionID), and because the peer's Reflector keys anti-replay
868		// AND the session challenge PER PathID, the strict-monotonic replay filter dropped one
869		// of the two independent ProbeSeq streams -> probe loss / false-DOWN. Deriving the
870		// high-water from the (prober-stamped) ps.id covers both the probe-transport case and
871		// the T12 no-prober case (where ps.id == i, so the high-water is len(m.paths)).
872		m.openPort = port
873		for _, ps := range m.paths {
874			if uint16(ps.id)+1 > m.nextPathID {
875				m.nextPathID = uint16(ps.id) + 1
876			}
877		}
878		// A DEFERRED path's boot prober already OWNS its path-id stamp; raise the
879		// high-water past it too, so a runtime AddPath arriving while the path is still
880		// deferred cannot mint that reserved id and collide with it once T55 binds it.
881		for _, dp := range m.deferred {
882			if dp.prober != nil {
883				if uint16(dp.prober.PathID())+1 > m.nextPathID {
884					m.nextPathID = uint16(dp.prober.PathID()) + 1
885				}
886			}
887		}
888	
889		// Reconcile the scheduler's membership with the path slice just rebuilt from
890		// m.defs. A runtime AddPath/RemovePath (T30) keeps m.defs, m.probers, AND the
891		// live scheduler in lockstep during an Open span; re-pinning the scheduler's
892		// health list to the BOUND probers HERE — index-aligned with m.paths, since each
893		// bound path appended its ps.prober to boundProbers in order above — is the single
894		// reconciliation point that makes that runtime membership survive this
895		// Close→Open cycle without a scheduler/path desync (no frozen zombie health
896		// entry, no resurrected removed path). A DEFERRED path (not-yet-assignable
897		// source_addr) is deliberately EXCLUDED here: its prober is absent from the
898		// scheduler, so Pick can never select it, and Send's Pick->m.paths[idx] mapping
899		// stays index-aligned with the bound-only path slice. Only meaningful with the
900		// probe transport: a bind without probers cannot change membership at runtime
901		// (AddPath is refused) and never defers, so its scheduler is left exactly as built.
902		// Reconcile EACH bound peer's scheduler membership with the path slice just rebuilt from
903		// m.defs (T93): every peer's per-(peer,path) views were appended in m.defs order above, so a
904		// peer's scheduler health list is that peer's BOUND probers in order (a DEFERRED path
905		// contributed no view and is deliberately excluded, exactly as for the primary). On the
906		// single-peer edge/hub m.peers holds only the primary, so this reconciles exactly the one
907		// scheduler — byte-identical to the pre-split single reconcile. A peer without the probe
908		// transport or with a non-dynamic scheduler is left as built (the T12 no-prober unit binds
909		// never change membership at runtime and never defer).
910		for _, p := range m.peers {
911			pdyn, pdynOK := p.scheduler.(sched.DynamicScheduler)
912			if p.probers == nil || !pdynOK {
913				continue
914			}
915			health := make([]sched.PathHealth, 0, len(p.paths))
916			for _, pp := range p.paths {
917				if pp.prober != nil {
918					health = append(health, pp.prober)
919				}
920			}
921			if err := pdyn.SetPaths(health); err != nil {
922				_ = m.closeSocketsLocked()
923				return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("bind: reconcile scheduler on open: %w", err)
924			}
925		}
926	
927		// Stand up the receive fan-in: one Bind-owned reader per path feeding the shared
928		// resequencer, and a single engine-facing drainer. Both channels are recreated
929		// per Open so a Close→Open cycle starts clean.
930		m.deliverSignal = make(chan struct{}, 1)
931		m.recvClosed = make(chan struct{})
932		for _, ps := range m.paths {
933			m.readersWG.Add(1)
934			go m.readLoop(ps, m.deliverSignal)
935		}
936		// Deadline-tick goroutine for FEC group close (T24): it flushes a partial group's
937		// parity on time under low load so a group is never stranded waiting for the size
938		// threshold. Tracked by readersWG (like the readers) so Close waits for it; it
939		// exits on recvClosed. It TryLocks m.mu, so it never blocks Close's readersWG.Wait
940		// held under m.mu.
941		if m.fecSend.Load() != nil {
942			m.readersWG.Add(1)
943			go m.fecTickLoop(m.fecCfg.Deadline, m.recvClosed)
944		}
945		return []ReceiveFunc{m.newReceiveFunc(m.deliverSignal, m.recvClosed)}, actualPort, nil
946	}
947	
948	// openPeerDatapathLocked (re)builds ONE peer's per-Open datapath planes: its send Codec
949	// (derived from THIS peer's psk), its receive resequencer, and — when FEC is configured —
950	// its FEC send/receive planes. Each is fresh per Open so a Close→Open cycle (or reconnect)
951	// re-pins the peer's release point and re-anchors its FEC grouping to this bring-up rather
952	// than a stale prior span. It writes ONLY the given peer's fields (from the peer's own psk
953	// and the bind-wide fecCfg/adaptiveCfg), so one peer's (re)creation never touches another
954	// peer's resequencer or FEC group state — the per-peer lifecycle boundary that keeps a
955	// reconnect on one peer from disturbing another's. Caller holds m.mu; on error the caller
956	// unwinds the whole Open via closeSocketsLocked (which clears every peer's per-Open state),
957	// so a partial build here is cleaned up.
958	func (m *Multipath) openPeerDatapathLocked(ps *peerState) error {
959		sendCodec, err := ps.newCodec()
960		if err != nil {
961			return err
962		}
963	
964		// A fresh resequencer per Open: its release point re-pins to the first frame THIS peer
965		// receives after this bring-up, so a Close→Open cycle (or reconnect) never wedges on a
966		// stale high-water outer-seq. Published atomically so the peer's per-path readLoop
967		// goroutines read it WITHOUT m.mu.
968		ps.resequencer.Store(reseq.New(resequencerWindow, resequencerTimeout, reseq.SystemClock{}))
969	
970		// Fresh FEC send/receive state per Open, when FEC is enabled (T24). The encoder
971		// group state and the decoder's per-group buffers re-pin with the sockets, so a
972		// Close→Open cycle never reconstructs against a stale group. Both are torn down (per
973		// peer) in closeSocketsLocked. A build error here is a programmer error (the ratio was
974		// validated in NewMultipath), so it fails the Open.
975		if m.fecCfg != nil {
976			fs, err := m.newFECSender()
977			if err != nil {
978				return err
979			}
980			ps.fecSend.Store(fs)
981			fr, err := m.newFECReceiver()
982			if err != nil {
983				return err
984			}
985			ps.fecRecv.Store(fr)
986		}
987		ps.sendCodec = sendCodec
988		return nil
989	}
990	
991	// newFECSender builds a fresh FEC send plane (group encoder + optional adaptive controller)
992	// from the bind-wide fecCfg/adaptiveCfg, or returns (nil, nil) when FEC is disabled. Like
993	// newFECReceiver it reads only immutable post-construction config, so it is safe to call
994	// WITHOUT m.mu — which the lock-free re-instantiation on re-bind (ensurePeerReceiveInstantiated)
995	// relies on to rebuild a torn-down concentrator peer's send plane symmetric to the receive
996	// plane. Open calls it too, so a lazily-rebuilt fecSend is byte-identical to an eagerly-opened
997	// one: a fresh encoder re-pinned to this bring-up, and in adaptive mode a fresh controller
998	// starting the control law from M=0 (no standing redundancy until loss is observed). A build
999	// error is a programmer error (the ratio/controller cfg were validated in NewMultipath).
1000	func (m *Multipath) newFECSender() (*fecSender, error) {
1001		if m.fecCfg == nil {
1002			return nil, nil
1003		}
1004		enc, err := fec.NewEncoder(*m.fecCfg, fec.SystemClock{})
1005		if err != nil {
1006			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bind: build FEC encoder: %w", err)
1007		}
1008		fs := &fecSender{enc: enc}
1009		if m.adaptiveCfg != nil {
1010			ctrl, err := adaptivefec.NewController(*m.adaptiveCfg, adaptivefec.SystemClock{})
1011			if err != nil {
1012				return nil, fmt.Errorf("bind: build adaptive FEC controller: %w", err)
1013			}
1014			fs.ctrl = ctrl
1015			// Adopt the controller's starting parity so encoder and controller agree from t=0; the
1016			// tick loop sizes it to measured loss within the first control interval. Fixed mode
1017			// leaves the encoder at its cfg.ParityShards default instead.
1018			enc.SetParity(ctrl.Parity())
